name: libtext-recordparser-perl
title: libtext-recordparser-perl
summary: module to parse record-oriented data in a text file
description:
module to parse record-oriented data in a text file
Text::RecordParser is a Perl module that extracts record-oriented data from
arbitrary text files. The most common example has records separated by
newlines and fields delimited by commas or tabs. This module aims to provide
a consistent interface for processing an arbitrary sequence of records in a
file, however they may be delimited. The data can then be extracted using
arrays or hashes.

commands.name: tab2graph
commands.description: turn tabular data into a graph

commands.name: tablify
commands.description: turn a delimited text file into a text table

commands.name: tabmerge
commands.description: unify delimited files on common fields

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use tab2graph because module to parse record-oriented data in a text file.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use tab2graph as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `tab2graph [options] file.tab` as documented upstream for tab2graph.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair tab2graph with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `tab2graph --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
